title: Surenus toona
taxonomy: Surenus toona is classified as a species of plant in the genus Toona, which is part of the family Meliaceae. The family Meliaceae is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the mahogany family. This classification places Surenus toona within the broader context of tropical and subtropical trees. The plant's classification is further supported by its characteristics, such as its large leaves and small, fragrant flowers. By understanding the classification of Surenus toona, researchers can better comprehend its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants in the Meliaceae family.
uses: Surenus toona, also known as the Toona tree, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The leaves and bark of the tree are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's extracts have also been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain and inflammation. In addition, the Toona tree is believed to have antiviral properties, which can aid in the treatment of viral infections. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Surenus toona.

intro:
There is no information on a plant called Surenus toona. However, Toona is a genus of trees in the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the mahogany family. Toona is a deciduous tree that is native to Asia and Australia. 

Based on the closest information available, the Toona tree, Toona ciliata, has been reported to have potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Its leaves, bark, and seeds are rich in bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids.

The health benefits of Toona are thought to be attributed to its bioactive constituents, which have been reported to have antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The most common herbal preparations made from Toona include teas, extracts, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
